Ground_Truth: Be vigilant and cautious; in ancient times, it was the origin of the character '敬,' representing an attitude of reverence.
LLM_Output: If; provided that; by chance; carelessly/roughly. Phono-semantic: from grass (艸) + 句 (phonetic). The early form resembles sprouting grass above a curved hook shape (句), likely first naming a plant and later borrowed to express a provisional, non-meticulous attitude and the conditional “if.”

Ground_Truth: foot; in ancient times, it also meant to assist, record, or serve as a phonetic loan for other characters such as 胥 (xū), 疏 (shū), 雎 (jū)
LLM_Output: Human foot or footprint. The graph depicts a long sole with toes, indicating the foot and the action of treading/stepping. Later loaned to mean a bolt of cloth (one 疋), hence also a measure/classifier.

Ground_Truth: Pick the fruits or leaves from the tree by hand; pluck the fruits or leaves from the tree with hands
LLM_Output: Picking or plucking; gather. Depicts a hand above a tree/branch with fruits, showing the action of taking fruit or leaves, extended to mean collect or select.

Ground_Truth: Pick the fruits or leaves from the tree by hand; pluck the fruits or leaves from the tree with hands
LLM_Output: Pluck or gather. From hand/claw (爫) over tree (木), depicting a hand reaching down to pick fruit from a tree; extended to mean take, collect, or select.

Ground_Truth: By the water; where the water meets the grass
LLM_Output: 河岸、水边之地。左为水的曲线，象流水；右为“眉”，本象眼上之眉，取声并以眉之“边际”意会水之边缘，表音“mei”。合意指水的边沿，即河畔，多见于水名或地名。

Ground_Truth: When the sun sets; in the evening
LLM_Output: Sun sinking behind grass. From two sprigs of grass above + the sun (日) below, depicting the sun hidden at sunset, hence dusk/dark; later loaned to mean “do not; none.”

Ground_Truth: When the sun sets; in the evening
LLM_Output: Sunset or evening. From grass above covering the sun below, picturing the sun sinking behind vegetation at day’s end, thus meaning dusk/twilight; later extended to the sense “do not.”

Ground_Truth: When the sun sets; in the evening
LLM_Output: Dusk, sunset. From sun 日 encircled by grass 艸艸, depicting the sun sinking into vegetation and light fading; later extended to mean “none; do not.”

Ground_Truth: When the sun sets; in the evening
LLM_Output: Dusk or darkness. From sun (日) surrounded by plants/trees (木), depicting the sun sinking into vegetation with light obscured; later borrowed as a negator meaning “do not, none.”

Ground_Truth: When the sun sets; in the evening
LLM_Output: Sun sinking beneath two trees, depicting the sun obscured by a forest, indicating dusk/darkness. By extension, “none; nothing; no one” and used as a prohibitive “do not.”

Ground_Truth: Clumps of trees, specifically a plant called "Jing"; also refers to the ancient state of Chu, named for its abundance of thorns; later extended to meanings such as clear and orderly (e.g., "清楚"), or suffering (e.g., "苦楚").
LLM_Output: Thicket of thorny trees or brushwood. From two trees (木木) flanking a central foot/疋 phonetic, picturing a person amid dense woods. Extended to the actions of cutting/clearing brush and to caning as punishment; later used as the name of the state of Chu.

Ground_Truth: Clumps of trees, specifically a plant called "Jing"; also refers to the ancient state of Chu, named for its abundance of thorns; later extended to meanings such as clear and orderly (e.g., "清楚"), or suffering (e.g., "苦楚").
LLM_Output: Thorny bush, dense thicket. The graph shows several forked twig/branch shapes clustered around a central area, picturing brambles. Extended meanings: to clear/beat brush, to flog with a wooden rod; later a state name (Chu).

Ground_Truth: disaster; fault; blame
LLM_Output: Fault or blame; misfortune from a wrong move. From 匕 (person/leaning figure) + 夂 (step/going), depicting a person taking a misdirected or backward step, hence error and guilt.

Ground_Truth: Originally referring to the patterns carved on jade; later denoting meticulousness or comprehensiveness; also referring to the Zhou Dynasty or a surrounding circle.
LLM_Output: Surrounding perimeter or enclosure. Resembling a frame that encases the four quarters, expressing “around on all sides,” hence the meanings encircle, go around, and by extension complete or thorough; later also used as the name of the Zhou dynasty.

Ground_Truth: end; the entire process from beginning to end; death; after all; whole, entire
LLM_Output: End, finish, completion. The form shows two thread ends with knots hanging and meeting at the top, indicating a thread tied off—work brought to a close; by extension, final stage or life’s end.

Ground_Truth: face, facial; also refers to the direction one is facing or oriented towards
LLM_Output: 人脸、正面。象人头之形，外轮廓为面部，内点与尖形为眼与鼻，故本义为脸面；引申为表面、方面，并有“面对”的动作义。

Ground_Truth: The beginning or end of something, such as the tip of a plant's root or the top of a person's head or the soles of their feet; later, it often refers to the meaning of 'specialized,' such as the phrase 'sincerely yours' at the end of a letter.
LLM_Output: Peak or extremity. Resembling a pointed mountain top, indicating the tip or utmost end; by extension, proper/upright (as in 端).

Ground_Truth: Water overflows from the container; later, it was extended to mean increase, benefit, or advantage.
LLM_Output: Increase, addition, benefit. Pictograph of a dish-shaped vessel with liquid rising and overflowing, indicating things becoming more and yielding surplus.

Ground_Truth: expedition or military campaign; later also referring to taxation
LLM_Output: Military campaign or punitive expedition; marching forth to attack. From 彳 (steps, movement) + 正 (set right/upright), depicting advancing to “correct” wrongdoing. By extension, to go out to levy or demand (e.g., taxes).

Ground_Truth: The name of a tree, also called Pyrus betulifolia or Tang crabapple; used as the name of a state in ancient times
LLM_Output: Tree growing from the ground; originally the name of a tree species. From 土 (earth) + 木 (tree), picturing a tree rooted in soil. Later extended to mean blocking or plugging (using wood to stop an opening), and also used as a surname.

Ground_Truth: the seventh of the Ten Heavenly Stems; to denote a road, repayment, continuation, or age
LLM_Output: Seventh Heavenly Stem, a calendrical day sign. Oracle form resembles a person bearing ripe grain, indicating ripening and change; by extension marks the autumn/metal phase and the “geng” day in the sexagenary cycle.

Ground_Truth: The time of dusk when the moon has just risen after the sun has set;
LLM_Output: Evening or night. Pictograph of a crescent moon appearing at dusk, indicating the time after sunset.

Ground_Truth: Approach food to prepare for a meal; extended meaning: near; immediately
LLM_Output: Approach or go to; immediate/namely. From 皀 (food vessel) + 卩 (kneeling person), depicting a person kneeling by a food container to take food, hence “go near/attend to,” later extended to “right away; namely.”

Ground_Truth: an ancient cart pulled by people; later also referring to the carriage used by the emperor or empress
LLM_Output: Man-powered carriage; to draw or carry a cart. From 車 (wheeled vehicle) below, with two components above depicting two people at a crossbar, showing two men pulling a wheeled cart. Later specifically the emperor’s small hand-drawn carriage (御輦), and by extension the action of pulling or carrying a v...

Ground_Truth: Items obtained through hunting;
LLM_Output: 捕获、取得。甲骨形合手（𠂇）与父，像手持器物伸出擒拿之状，表示在狩猎中抓住猎物，后引申为得到、所得之物。

Ground_Truth: Items obtained through hunting;
LLM_Output: Capture or obtain. From a hand (又) seizing a short‑tailed bird (隹), picturing catching a bird; extended from hunting capture to the general sense of getting, winning, or harvesting.

Ground_Truth: Exhale through the mouth; play a wind instrument; wind flows; boast; things fail
LLM_Output: Blow or exhale. From mouth + 欠 (breath), resembling a mouth expelling air and a person breathing out, meaning to blow air, make wind, or produce sound by blowing (e.g., on pipes).

Ground_Truth: The appearance of ghosts or spirits, possibly referring to a ghost with disheveled hair;
LLM_Output: Evil spirit or mountain demon that deludes or harms people. From 鬼 + 彡, depicting a ghost with bristling hair/stripes; 彡 marks its eerie appearance. Later used as the same as “魅”.

Ground_Truth: Hold something under the arm; or have people on both sides help together
LLM_Output: 夹持、挟在中间之意。甲骨文像一人居中（大），两侧各有屈臂之手（或两人）向内合拢，象两边相向按住之形，故表示夹、挟、夹击，亦指处于两者之间。

Ground_Truth: Hold something under the arm; or have people on both sides help together
LLM_Output: Clamp or press from both sides; to insert between or pick up (as with chopsticks). Pictograph of a large figure in the center (天/大) being held by two side strokes (尼-like), showing two sides squeezing a middle object.

Ground_Truth: Hunting with hounds and tools; later also referring to the hunted animals
LLM_Output: Wild beast, ferocious animal. From 單 + 犬: 犬 marks the animal/beast category, 單 provides the sound and depicts a head with raised horns/ears. The oracle form shows a horned head over a body with a canine form, portraying a fierce quadruped. Refers to large wild game and savage animals, often in hunti...

Ground_Truth: In ancient times, it referred to the demarcation of land boundaries or the conferment of titles; later, it also denoted sealing, tombs, or imperial sacrifices to heaven.
LLM_Output: Boundary mound or marker. Resembling a stake/plant with branching top planted in the ground, indicating marking and enclosing land; later extended to enfeoff (grant territory) and to seal/close.

Ground_Truth: feces; excrement; stool
LLM_Output: Excrement; the act of defecating. From 尸 (body) with small dotted strokes (小) below, depicting feces dropping from the body; refers to human or animal feces and, by extension, to defecation.

Ground_Truth: an ancient long-handled ladle for scooping water; a unit of capacity (ten liters equal one dou); the Big Dipper (a star group shaped like a ladle)
LLM_Output: Measuring ladle (dou). Pictograph of a handled scoop for grain or wine, indicating the action of scooping/measuring; later extended to the capacity unit “dou.”

Ground_Truth: Press with the hand on a person or object; extended meanings include seal, printing, impression, etc.
LLM_Output: Seal and stamping action. From 卩 + 夂, resembling a downward motion pressing a small seal block, indicating the act of impressing a mark; later extended to official seal, imprint, proof of authority, and to print.

Ground_Truth: Originally referring to going in and out of a basement; later extended to mean repetition, restoration, reply, etc.
LLM_Output: Return; again; repeat. Oracle form shows a person with legs/footprints turning back, depicting the action of retracing one’s steps and restoring a prior state; later extended to recovery and repetition. Also used as phonetic fu in compounds (e.g., 腹).

Ground_Truth: female or girl
LLM_Output: Woman; female. A pictograph of a kneeling figure with bent legs and crossed arms on the chest, depicting a woman; later extended to female-related roles such as wife or daughter.

Ground_Truth: female or girl
LLM_Output: Female, woman. Pictograph of a kneeling woman with crossed arms; the two short side strokes mark the breasts, indicating female identity.

Ground_Truth: Wisdom gained through learning and imparting knowledge
LLM_Output: Wisdom or intelligent understanding. From 子 (child) over 冊 (bamboo slips/books), resembling a child studying bamboo slips; by extension, learning and organized knowledge that yield insight, good judgment, and resourcefulness.

Ground_Truth: son's son; descendants
LLM_Output: Grandchild; descendant; later generations. From child (子) + fine/twisted thread (幺/糸). The left depicts a child; the right resembles a coiled silk thread, symbolizing slender, continuing lineage—hence the younger offspring of one’s child and, by extension, posterity.

Ground_Truth: Delicious; tasty food
LLM_Output: Delicious taste; to taste food. From spoon (匕) above mouth (口), depicting bringing a spoon to the mouth to sample food; later extended to gist/intention and decree.

Ground_Truth: Rotate; transport; type or indicate plate
LLM_Output: Moving or turning a boat with a hand-held pole. From boat + action-hand (like 攴/殳), depicting pushing and rotating a boat; extended to mean move, transfer, and later “various; all kinds.”

Ground_Truth: Originally referring to accompanying someone in drinking; later extended to mean two things that match well together, such as a spouse or a blend.
LLM_Output: Pairing, matching, or assigning. From wine jar (酉) + kneeling person (卩), depicting presenting/apportioning wine to someone in ritual, hence the meanings “to match, to accompany/associate,” and by extension “spouse” and “to distribute/assign.”

Ground_Truth: alone; a measure word for birds or one of a pair of items
LLM_Output: Single, one individual; a classifier for birds and some animals. Pictograph of a hand seizing a short‑tailed bird, resembling a grasped bird, originally meaning “to catch one,” later extended to “single; solitary.”

Ground_Truth: Cut open the ox horn with a knife; extended meanings include separate, dismantle, understand, relax
LLM_Output: Disjointing a horned animal. From 角 (horns) + 丰 (the animal’s body) + 𦥑 (hand/knife indicating the cutting action), depicting cutting a horned beast at its joints. Original meaning: to cut up or disarticulate; extended to untie, loosen, relieve; later further extended to explain or solve.

Ground_Truth: ugly; disgusting; shameful; embarrassing
LLM_Output: Ugly, abominable, or disgraceful appearance; also extended to “clown.” From wine jar (酉) + ghost (鬼), depicting a ghostly figure associated with drink, likening a drunken, distorted face to a ghoul—hence the sense of unsightly and shameful.

Ground_Truth: Take something with hands or obtain something, such as withdrawing money or picking up a package; in ancient warfare, cutting off enemies' ears to count, later generalized to mean acquiring
LLM_Output: Take or seize. From ear (耳) + hand (又), depicting a hand grasping an ear—reflecting the wartime act of cutting off an enemy’s ear to tally merit; extended to mean take, obtain, capture, or select.

Ground_Truth: Tie the animals caught from hunting with ropes and raise them in the fields; later, it refers to domesticated or raised animals.
LLM_Output: Livestock; to raise and keep animals in an enclosure. The upper part resembles a tied cord or tuft of a domesticated animal, the lower part like a penned field divided into quarters with dots for animals, depicting beasts confined and nurtured. Hence “to domesticate; livestock.”

Ground_Truth: An axe used for cutting wood in ancient times; later also used as a unit of weight (the "jin" in one jin or two jin)
LLM_Output: Axe, a chopping tool. Oracle-bone form depicts an axe head with a short handle, indicating the action of cutting or chopping; later also borrowed as the weight unit “jin.”

Ground_Truth: depressed and unhappy; also refers to the lush appearance of vegetation
LLM_Output: Luxuriant density and congestion. The graph shows two trees (林) above, an enclosing wrap (勹) below, with a flare-like sign (尞) for rising vapors—like aroma or breath. It depicts vapors trapped in thick woods, thus “dense, overgrown,” and by extension “blocked, pent‑up, gloomy/depressed.”

Ground_Truth: depressed and unhappy; also refers to the lush appearance of vegetation
LLM_Output: Luxuriant, densely clustered trees; a thicket or forest. From multiple trees crowding together, showing growth that accumulates and does not disperse; extended to an accumulated, blocked state—stifled, oppressive, or depressed feelings, and also rich fragrance gathering.

Ground_Truth: Castrated boar; or the way a pig moves with its feet tied
LLM_Output: Pig (boar). Pictograph of a pig in profile with snout, bristles, legs, and tail, representing swine and pig-related matters.

Ground_Truth: The elderly or aged things; the image of white hair, hunched back, and walking with a cane
LLM_Output: 老人、年老。象形：上为鬓发，下为身躯，旁垂短画为手，长画为拐杖；描绘弯腰持杖而行之人，表示衰老之状。引申为年长者、长辈，亦指资深与尊称“老”。

Ground_Truth: Newly hatched birds or chicks
LLM_Output: Young bird, chick; fledgling, hence immature/young. From bird (隹) indicating “bird” and 芻 as phonetic (also hinting fodder). The graph shows a short‑tailed bird beside sprouting grass/fodder, picturing a chick that needs feeding.

Ground_Truth: children of siblings; such as nephews, nieces
LLM_Output: Niece, specifically a brother’s daughter. From 女 + 至, a phonetic-semantic compound: 女 indicates female; 至 gives the sound zhí. The oracle form shows a woman beside 至, denoting a female junior of one’s patrilineal kin—i.e., niece.

Ground_Truth: It refers to the stem of a plant; an obstacle or pause; a sneeze; or the fundamental meaning, such as the stem of a fruit; being tripped while walking; a sudden sneeze; it can also represent the foundation of things.
LLM_Output: 绊倒、躓足，行走受阻之义。上像突起带刺的障碍物，下像足，象足触物被绊而停滞，故表示被堵塞、不能前进。

Ground_Truth: Poultry chicken; an animal that crows at dawn
LLM_Output: 家禽雞、公雞。象形，像頂著雞冠、伸喙垂翼、長尾利爪的鳥形，指雞本身，並引申為雞鳴、報曉、拂曉。

Ground_Truth: Poultry chicken; an animal that crows at dawn
LLM_Output: Rooster (domestic chicken). A pictograph of a crested bird with beak, wing, and claws; combined with a head element (頁) to emphasize the comb, with 奚 as phonetic, denoting the household fowl that crows at dawn.

Ground_Truth: the flesh of animals; later also referring to human muscles or flesh
LLM_Output: Meat or flesh. Pictograph of a cut slab of meat with inner lines marking muscle and fat; extended to the body and internal organs, and by extension meat as food.

Ground_Truth: Tasks or obligations to be undertaken (such as responsibilities); originally referred to taxes levied in ancient times
LLM_Output: Demand payment or levy fines; debt and liability; by extension responsibility/blame. From 朿 (thorn) over 貝 (cowry money), like pricking to press for cowries, meaning to exact payment and hold someone to account.

Ground_Truth: Ancient sacrificial rituals using music and dance to pray for rain;
LLM_Output: Rain-prayer ritual. From rain above + woman/shaman below, resembling a person dancing under rain clouds to invoke rainfall; refers to the ancient ceremony for praying for rain (大雩), extended to praying for relief from drought.

Ground_Truth: Land for growing crops; in ancient times, also referred to as hunting grounds
LLM_Output: Field or farmland. Pictograph of a square plot divided by cross-paths into four sections, indicating cultivated land and its boundary lines.

Ground_Truth: Two pigs together may be pigs chasing or fighting each other; it could also refer to a group of pigs running around wildly; in ancient times, it was also used to mean stubborn or aggressive.
LLM_Output: Herd of pigs; many swine. From two豕 placed side by side, resembling two pigs walking together, indicating a group or plurality of pigs.

Ground_Truth: buttocks; the part of the body one sits on
LLM_Output: Buttocks, the rear of the human/animal body. Oracle form resembles a torso in profile with a protruding hip/haunch. Later as a phono-semantic from flesh (肉/月) indicating a body part + 殿 as phonetic, referring to the hip/buttock area and by extension the rump or sitting on the rear.

Ground_Truth: To hold up or receive something with both hands; extended to mean undertake, inherit, or bear.
LLM_Output: Receive or support with both hands. From hand (又) plus phonetic 丞, the graph shows two hands below holding up an object, indicating the action of lifting, bearing, undertaking; extended to inherit or continue.

Ground_Truth: fawn or young deer; also refers to a fierce beast similar to a lion
LLM_Output: Fawn (young deer). From deer + 兒 (phonetic), the oracle form depicts a small deer with head, ears/antlers and legs, referring to a young deer.

Ground_Truth: An ancient long-handled weapon made of bamboo or wood, with angular edges at the top; used for striking or defense.
LLM_Output: 手持杖棒之形，象以手执兵器击打。表示执器击、攻、擣之意，后作声义部件，指与持器击斫、敲击相关之事（如聲、芟、磬）。

Ground_Truth: Places where people live; also refers to families or professionals
LLM_Output: Home or household. From a roof (宀) covering a pig (豕), depicting a house where domesticated pigs are kept, signifying a settled dwelling and the family living there; later extended to mean family/lineage, residence, and by extension a specialist or master (suffix “-ist”).

Ground_Truth: Places where people live; also refers to families or professionals
LLM_Output: Home or household. Pictograph of a roof enclosing a pig (豭/豕), resembling a pig kept under a roof—i.e., a dwelling where people live and raise livestock; hence “house, family.” Later extended to family/lineage and to “a person of a certain profession” (suffix -家).

Ground_Truth: Artificially cultivated poisonous insects in ancient legends, used to harm people; also refers to disasters or being bewitched
LLM_Output: Worms in a vessel; food spoilage and decay. From 皿 (container) with 虫 (insect) inside, picturing wriggling bugs in a bowl, hence putrefaction, corruption; later extended to gu-poison and harmful witchcraft.

Ground_Truth: Artificially cultivated poisonous insects in ancient legends, used to harm people; also refers to disasters or being bewitched
LLM_Output: Insects bred in a vessel. From 皿 (dish) + two 虫 (worms), depicting worms in a bowl; originally the gu poison/sorcery made by raising insects to harm, extended to infestation, spoilage, and corruption.

Ground_Truth: bent things; also refers to tunes in music
LLM_Output: Bent or curved. Pictograph of an L-shaped, sectioned object (like a bent wooden strip or cornered path), indicating bending/turning; extended to mean crooked or winding, and later loaned for “melody/tune.”

Ground_Truth: yawning; lack or shortage; owe; bow slightly to show respect
LLM_Output: Yawning or exhaling. Pictograph of a person with an open mouth and bent body, showing breath flowing out; extended to mean lack, deficiency, or owe.

Ground_Truth: A place for hiding or demarcating; also used as a place name or surname
LLM_Output: Region or division; to demarcate and separate. The graph shows an enclosure (like a box, 匚) containing multiple units (three 口), picturing compartments within a bounded space, hence “to partition, distinguish,” and by extension “district, section, zone.”

Ground_Truth: An ancient sacrificial activity; later also used to mean resisting or defending.
LLM_Output: Ritual warding off and defense. From 示 (altar/ritual) plus components 卩 (kneeling person) and 午 (pestle/weapon), depicting a kneeling person brandishing a tool before an altar to repel harm; hence the action “to ward off, exorcise, guard against, resist.”

Ground_Truth: married woman; wife
LLM_Output: Married woman, wife; adult woman managing the household. From woman + broom, depicting a woman holding a broom, indicating domestic duties and the role of the house mistress; later extended to general “woman”.

Ground_Truth: meet or come across on the road; encounter or run into
LLM_Output: Meet or encounter on the road. From the walking foot 夂 (movement along a path) + 夆 (phonetic, from 丰, “face one another”), the form depicts two parties approaching and coming together, hence to meet, run into, or happen upon (including encountering a time or opportunity).

Ground_Truth: Many people gather together; extended to mean a large quantity or the common people.
LLM_Output: Crowd, multitude; many people gathered. The graph shows an eye (目) above three person-shapes (人人人), picturing “many under the gaze of many,” hence the meaning “the masses; numerous.”

Ground_Truth: Weapons or military-related matters; also refers to ancient terms for foreign ethnic groups
LLM_Output: Weapons and warfare; the army. Pictograph of a shield (like 盾) set with a dagger-axe, showing battle gear, hence meaning arms and military affairs (later also used for the Rong people).

Ground_Truth: Patterns embroidered on clothing with needle and thread; in ancient times, specifically referred to symmetrical designs with two opposing elements; later generalized to decorative embroidery.
LLM_Output: Embroidery and needlework; to stitch patterns on cloth. Pictograph of a needle with thread zigzagging across cloth edges, indicating the action of sewing decorative designs.

Ground_Truth: Entertain guests with wine and food; in ancient times, it referred to offering food during feasts or sacrifices.
LLM_Output: Feast and offering food. From 食 (food) flanked by two 卩 (kneeling persons), resembling two kneeling participants facing a central food vessel, indicating presenting and sharing food and drink in ritual or banquet; extended to entertain guests.

Ground_Truth: insatiable greed; excessive pursuit of wealth or profit
LLM_Output: Greed, covetous desire. From 女 + 林 (two 木), visually a woman beneath two trees; 女 marks emotion/desire, 林 provides the sound lán. In ancient use, denotes excessive wanting or craving, hence “greedy.”

Ground_Truth: a grass used for weaving mats and sandals; also refers to flower buds or lush vegetation; can also mean to wrap or encompass
LLM_Output: Plant bud wrapped by leaves. From grass (艸) above and wrap (包) below, picturing a sprout enclosed in its sheath; extended to mean bract, husk, and the act of wrapping or enclosing.

Ground_Truth: Hard natural minerals; also used as a unit of weight (120 jin)
LLM_Output: Stone or rock. Pictograph of a boulder at the foot of a cliff, representing hard natural minerals; later also a unit of weight (one shi = 120 jin).

Ground_Truth: Hard natural minerals; also used as a unit of weight (120 jin)
LLM_Output: Stone or rock. Pictograph of a rock under a cliff—the left block depicts a lump of stone, the right slanted pillar shows the cliff—hence “hard natural minerals”; later also a unit of weight (one shi = 120 jin).

Ground_Truth: ancient term for shield; later extended to mean defense, offense, or interference
LLM_Output: Shield. Pictograph of a handheld shield with a central grip (vertical stroke) and crosspiece, the forked top outlining the shield’s rim; extended to the action of warding off, blocking, or opposing.

Ground_Truth: Holding a food container with both hands and ascending to a high place; later, it generally refers to actions such as rising, climbing, or recording.
LLM_Output: Ascend or climb. From advancing feet (癶) above a high-footed sacrificial vessel (豆), depicting stepping up to place offerings on the vessel; hence meanings of going up, boarding, and by extension presenting/offerings and promotion.

Ground_Truth: Holding a food container with both hands and ascending to a high place; later, it generally refers to actions such as rising, climbing, or recording.
LLM_Output: Ascend, climb, or board; also to place offerings on a raised tray, hence extension to promotion or elevation. From two “止” (feet) above “豆” (high-footed food vessel), picturing feet stepping onto a tall-stemmed dou; the oracle form shows ascent and placing offerings.

Ground_Truth: A group formed by the same bloodline or common goals, such as a family; a nation
LLM_Output: Clan or tribe; a group sharing the same bloodline or features, and by extension a military unit. From 㫃 (flag) + 矢 (arrow): the oracle form shows a banner on a pole and an arrow, indicating people gathered under a flag for warfare, hence “tribe/army group.”

Ground_Truth: A group formed by the same bloodline or common goals, such as a family; a nation
LLM_Output: Clan or tribe; a group of people sharing the same bloodline. From 㫃 (banner) + 矢 (arrow), resembling a war banner with arrows that rally and organize people, originally indicating a military unit assembled under a flag, later extended to a lineage or ethnic group.

Ground_Truth: A group formed by the same bloodline or common goals, such as a family; a nation
